findings.issue: InvitationPage fetch useEffect has no abort/cleanup guard — sets state on potentially unmounted component. InvitationCompletePage received active-flag + StrictMode ref cleanup in Round 2, but InvitationPage's own fetch (getInvitation) was left without equivalent cleanup, creating an inconsistency within the same workstream.
findings.remediation: Add an AbortController or active flag (const active = true; return () => { active = false }) mirroring the pattern applied in InvitationCompletePage.tsx:25-92.

findings.issue: No test covers the error path where redeemOAuth succeeds but acceptInvitation fails. The catch block at InvitationCompletePage.tsx:83-88 handles this case, and the mock for acceptInvitation exists, but the test suite only exercises the redeemOAuth rejection path (line 110-118) and success path, leaving a gap in error path coverage.
findings.remediation: Add a test case: set oauth_verifier + invitation_token in sessionStorage, mock redeemOAuth to resolve but acceptInvitation to reject, assert the error message from acceptInvitation is displayed and sessionStorage cleanup occurs.

findings.issue: LoginPage useEffect only pushes error state when urlError is truthy, but never clears when urlError becomes null (e.g. in-app navigation from /login?error=X to /login). Stale error persists after the URL error param is gone.
findings.remediation: Remove the conditional guard and always call setError(urlError) in the effect, or derive error directly from searchParams with useMemo and use a separate state for button-triggered errors.
